stimulus: Renowned cinematographer Conrad Hall was admired for his bold approach to natural light. While filming a desert sequence for the movie Cool Hand Luke ______blank Hall used reflectors instead of artificial lights. This allowed him to capture the harshness of the sun and give the scene a raw, lived-in quality.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The convention being tested is the use of punctuation to mark the boundary between a supplementary element and a main clause. A comma is needed after the parenthetical year "(1967)" to separate the introductory subordinate clause from the main clause that follows ("Hall used reflectors...").

Choice A is incorrect because adding "and" turns the structure into a coordination that doesn't fit the sentence. Choice B is incorrect for the same reason. Choice C is incorrect because no punctuation between the introductory subordinate clause and the main clause results in a confusing, ungrammatical sentence.
